stories.title: Monte Carlo SF Day — Sinner vs. Zverev + Alcaraz vs. Vacherot (No. 1 Ranking At Stake)
stories.angle: All four SF players confirmed; the day could set up a Sinner-Alcaraz No. 1 decider final on Sunday. Sinner leads H2H vs. Zverev 8-4 (won last 8 meetings); Alcaraz 25-1 on clay since last year's MC faces first-ever meeting vs. Vacherot. Ranking projection: Sinner wins title = returns to No. 1 (was 1,190 pts behind pre-tournament).

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Monte Carlo SFs today:

🎾 Sinner vs. Zverev (1:30 PM CEST) — H2H 8-4 Sinner
🎾 Alcaraz vs. Vacherot (3:30 PM CEST) — first ever meeting

Win the title, Sinner = back to No. 1.

Biggest day of the clay season. 🔥

#Tennis #MonteCarlo

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Sinner & Alcaraz are both 66 weeks into their careers as ATP No. 1.

Today's SFs decide who gets closer to breaking that tie.

If they both win → Sunday is a No. 1 decider final at Monte Carlo.

#Tennis #ATPTour

article.headline: No. 1 on the Line: Monte Carlo's Semifinal Saturday Could Rewrite the Rankings

image_concepts.label: Image 1A
image_concepts.usage: X + FB caption post — SF day preview

research.facts:
Monte Carlo SF Day — Sinner vs. Zverev + Alcaraz vs. Vacherot
**Sources:**
- atptour.com — "Monte-Carlo SFs: Schedule, who is playing, how to watch & more" (April 10)
- montecarlotennismasters.com — "SF Preview: Vacherot v Alcaraz, Sinner v Zverev"
- tennistonic.com — "PREDICTION, PREVIEW, H2H: Alcaraz, Vacherot, Sinner and Zverev to play on COURT RAINIER III on Saturday" (April 10)
- puntodebreak.com — "This is how the Monte Carlo 2026 semifinals will be: Alcaraz and Sinner, with the number one at stake" (April 10)
- atptour.com — "How Jannik Sinner can become World No. 1 in Monte-Carlo"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Sinner vs. Zverev scheduled 1:30 PM CEST; Alcaraz vs. Vacherot scheduled 3:30 PM CEST on Court Rainier III (2 sources)
- H2H Sinner vs. Zverev: 8-4 overall, 1-1 on clay; 4th consecutive Masters 1000 SF between the pair (tennistonic.com, atptour.com)
- Alcaraz 2026 record: 20-2, clay record 3-0 at MC (tennistonic.com)
- Sinner 2026 record: 22-2, clay record 3-0 at MC (tennistonic.com)
- Ranking gap pre-tournament: Sinner 1,190 pts behind Alcaraz; Sinner 0 pts to defend; Alcaraz defending 1,000 pts (atptour.com, multiple sources)
- Sinner wins title = returns to No. 1 (atptour.com official projection)
- Both Alcaraz and Sinner have spent exactly 66 weeks each atop ATP rankings in their careers (montecarlotennismasters.com)
- Alcaraz path to SF: def. Báez 6-1 6-3, Etcheverry 6-1 4-6 6-3, Bublik 6-3 6-0 (tennistonic.com)
- Sinner path to SF: def. Humbert 6-3 6-0, Machac 6-1 6-7(3) 6-3, FAA 6-3 6-4 (atptour.com)
- Zverev path to SF: def. Garin 4-6 6-4 7-5, Bergs 6-2 7-5, Fonseca 7-5 6-7(3) 6-3 (tennistonic.com)
**Freshness justification:** SF day coverage — matches scheduled today April 11. This is the biggest day of the tournament. The No. 1 ranking storyline is one of the defining narratives of the 2026 season. Never covered a MC SF day preview yet.

stories.title: Sinner's 20 Consecutive Masters 1000 Wins — Joins Big 3 Milestone
stories.angle: Only 4th player in ATP history to win 20+ consecutive Masters 1000 matches — joins Novak Djokovic, Roger Federer, Rafael Nadal. Sinner has won 3 consecutive M1000 titles (Paris, Indian Wells, Miami); 22-2 season record; streak began after 2025 ban.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Djokovic did it.
Federer did it.
Nadal did it.

Now Sinner has done it.

20 consecutive Masters 1000 wins — only the 4th man in ATP history to reach that mark.

And he's in the SF today. 🔥

#Tennis #Sinner

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Sinner's 20-match M1000 streak:
• Paris title ✓
• Indian Wells title ✓
• Miami title ✓
• Monte Carlo — SF today vs. Zverev

He's won 3 consecutive Masters titles in a row. The 4th would make him No. 1 again.

#Tennis #ATPTour

article.headline: Djokovic. Federer. Nadal. Sinner. How Jannik Joined the 20-Win Masters Club

research.facts:
Sinner's 20 Consecutive Masters 1000 Wins
**Sources:**
- atptour.com — "Jannik Sinner downs Auger-Aliassime in Monte-Carlo, matches 'Big 3' milestone" (April 10)
- skysports.com — "Monte-Carlo Masters: Jannik Sinner matches 'big three' milestone with 20th successive Masters 1000 win" (April 10)
- tennis.com — "Jannik Sinner extends Masters 1000 winning streak to 20 matches in a row, joins legendary list" (April 10)
- beinsports.com — "Sinner into Monte-Carlo semis after 20th straight win at ATP Masters 1000 events" (April 10)
- yardbarker.com — "'It was a step forward': Jannik Sinner extends 20-match Masters 1000 streak, sets up Zverev showdown" (April 10)
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Sinner def. FAA 6-3, 6-4 in MC QF to extend streak to 20 (atptour.com — primary source)
- Only 4th man to reach 20 consecutive M1000 wins — joins Djokovic, Federer, Nadal (atptour.com + skysports.com — 2 sources)
- Sinner's streak is "the seventh-longest in series history" (skysports.com / beinsports.com)
- Four preceding streaks all belonged to Djokovic (4), with one each from Federer and Nadal (skysports.com)
- Sinner won consecutive M1000 titles in Paris, Indian Wells, and Miami (multiple sources)
- Sinner missed 2025 Monte Carlo due to doping ban (established context, multiple sources)
- FAA score: 6-3, 6-4 (atptour.com primary source)
**Freshness justification:** Milestone reached April 10 in QF; context/depth article for April 11 appropriate. New STORY — not previously covered in story-history.md.

stories.title: Vacherot's Historic MC Semifinal — First Monegasque in Open Era
stories.angle: Valentin Vacherot (No. 23) became the first Monegasque player to reach the MC SF in the Open Era. Beat Cerundolo, Musetti (7-6(6), 7-5), Hurkacz, De Minaur. Now faces defending champ Alcaraz. Two five-set battles in a row — 5h17m combined — going into the SF.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Valentin Vacherot is the FIRST Monegasque player to reach the Monte Carlo Masters SF in the Open Era.

Playing at home. Coached by his half-brother. Beat Hurkacz and De Minaur to get here.

Now he faces defending champ Alcaraz. 🇲🇨

#Tennis #MonteCarlo

article.headline: Monaco's Own: Vacherot Makes History as First Monegasque MC Semifinalist in the Open Era

research.facts:
Vacherot's Historic MC Semifinal
**Sources:**
- tennismajors.com — "Vacherot stuns de Minaur to become the first Monégasque in a Monte-Carlo Masters semifinal" (April 10)
- atptour.com — "Valentin Vacherot continues historic run, saves 13 BPs en route to Monte-Carlo SFs" (April 10)
- montecarlotennismasters.com — SF Preview (April 10)
- tennis365.com — "Monte Carlo Masters: Valentin Vacherot makes rankings breakthrough before Carlos Alcaraz showdown" (April 11)
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- First Monegasque player to reach MC SF in the Open Era (tennismajors.com + atptour.com — 2 sources)
- Vacherot def. De Minaur in QF (score: covered April 10 — 6-7(4), 6-3, 6-4 from prior research)
- Vacherot def. Hurkacz 6-7(4), 6-3, 6-4 in 2h53m (story-history.md, verified April 10)
- Vacherot's QF run: beat Cerundolo, Musetti (7-6(6), 7-5), Hurkacz, De Minaur (atptour.com)
- Saved 13 break points en route to SFs (atptour.com primary source)
- Two combined QF + R3 battles lasted 5h17m total (montecarlotennismasters.com)
- Vacherot world No. 23 (tennis365.com — rankings milestone)
- Vacherot's half-brother Balleret is his coach (story-history.md, April 10)
- This is Vacherot's first-ever meeting with Alcaraz (tennistonic.com)
**Freshness justification:** QF win covered April 10, but new angle is the SF confirmation + historic first for Monaco + SF matchup preview vs. defending champion.

stories.title: Sabalenka Withdraws from Stuttgart — Rybakina Now No. 1 Seed
stories.angle: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka pulls out of the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix (Stuttgart, April 13-19) with injury sustained after Miami title. Elena Rybakina (No. 2) inherits top seed. Gauff and Swiatek also entered; Stuttgart draw to be revealed April 11. Eala got unexpected boost as Zheng Qinwen also withdrew.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Sabalenka is OUT of Stuttgart (starts April 13).

Injury after winning Miami. Rybakina moves to No. 1 seed.

Swiatek has won Stuttgart twice. Gauff is lurking. The draw just got wide open.

#Tennis #WTA #Stuttgart

article.headline: Sabalenka Out of Stuttgart — Rybakina Inherits Top Seed as WTA Clay Season Opens

image_concepts.label: Image 4B
image_concepts.usage: Article hero — Rybakina as new No. 1 seed

research.facts:
Sabalenka Withdraws from Stuttgart
**Sources:**
- wtatennis.com — "Sabalenka pulls out of Stuttgart with an injury" (official WTA source)
- tennis365.com — "2026 WTA Stuttgart Entry List" (April 2026)
- sports.yahoo.com — "Alex Eala handed unexpected Stuttgart Open boost as Qinwen Zheng pulls out" (April 2026)
- wtatennis.com — "Stuttgart 411: Dates, top players, withdrawals, prize money and more"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Sabalenka withdrew from Stuttgart with injury (wtatennis.com — official source)
- Injury sustained after winning Miami Open (context established)
- Magdalena Frech replaces Sabalenka in main draw (crushrushnews.com)
- Rybakina now No. 1 seed; Gauff No. 2; Swiatek No. 3 (tennis365.com, WTA insider tweet)
- Actual seeds per WTA: 1-Sabalenka, 2-Rybakina, 3-Gauff, 4-Swiatek, 5-Svitolina, 6-Paolini, 7-Andreeva, 8-Muchova (entry list — now shuffled up one with Sabalenka out)
- Stuttgart starts April 13; WTA 500 (not 1000); indoor clay at Porsche Arena (wtatennis.com)
- Draw revealed April 11 (wtatennis.com schedule)
- Qinwen Zheng also withdrew (yahoo sports) — Alex Eala enters main draw
**Freshness justification:** Sabalenka withdrawal is breaking news impacting Stuttgart favorites. Major disruption to the clay season's first big WTA event.

stories.title: WTA Linz Semifinals — Andreeva vs. Ruse + Potapova vs. Vekic
stories.angle: Mirra Andreeva (No. 1 seed) vs. Elena-Gabriela Ruse in SF1 — Ruse beat Ostapenko in QF (4-6, 6-4, 6-1); Andreeva beat Cirstea to reach semis. Anastasia Potapova (Austrian citizen, local angle) vs. Donna Vekic in SF2. Title match Sunday.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
WTA Linz SFs today:

🎾 Andreeva vs. Ruse (3:30 PM local) — No. 1 seed vs. the woman who just beat Ostapenko
🎾 Potapova vs. Vekic (6:00 PM local) — Austrian citizen vs. Croatian silver medalist

Final is Sunday. #Tennis #WTA

article.headline: Linz Semifinals Saturday: Andreeva Eyes Title, Potapova Plays for Austria
